reasoning: Mauritania has experienced very low levels of 'Violence against civilians' event types, with only ~4 such events in the last 360 days (producing a baseline reference value of 1.333). Reaching the threshold requires at least 14 events to occur in a 30-day window leading up to each resolution date. This represents an unprecedented 42x scale-up compared to the historic baseline. ACLED data and news sources show the country has extremely low internal violent activity and has not suffered major internal terrorist attacks/insurgencies since 2011. While Mali-border dynamics involving Wagner/FAMa create a minor spillover risk, registering 14 distinct events against civilians in a single month would require a massive sudden crisis. There is no evidence of an ongoing crisis as of late May 2025. Therefore, probabilities are set to the lowest allowed bound for the immediate resolution dates, increasing slightly for the end of the year to reflect longer forecast horizons.
